rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope when bindings depend on subcomponent ports or implementation-level state.
Place component-level assumptions and guarantees in the component type scope if they refer only to visible ports and concrete bounds.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL architecture integrity by only referencing ports, subcomponents, and connections explicitly declared in the input model.
Ensure AGREE annex scope matches the semantic level of the referenced elements: type scope for visible interfaces, implementation scope for internal bindings.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` with valid syntax for declarations and contracts.
Use only visible features (ports, properties) in contracts; avoid subcomponent paths in component type annexes.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Period, Deadline) as AGREE variables—they are not in expression scope.
Do not convert Boolean or enumeration ports to numeric comparisons unless the AADL model defines them as numeric scalars.
